vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear
Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance
Center pod must accommodate motor mount, bolt circle, cooling inlet, pusher prop, motor pod, fastener pattern, harness path

risk.desc: Potential service access issues due to complex harness routing.
risk.mit: Review harness routing during assembly to ensure accessibility.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient cooling airflow to motor pod.
risk.mit: Conduct airflow tests during design phase to validate cooling path.

risk.desc: Weak interface between wing spar and center body pod.
risk.mit: Increase bolt count and add reinforcing ribs at the interface.

risk.desc: Risk of process limitations affecting FDM build quality.
risk.mit: Conduct FDM build trials to validate material and process choices.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment of motor mount due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement precision alignment tools during assembly.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to varying thermal zones.
risk.mit: Implement thermal expansion joints and use materials with similar thermal expansion coefficients.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient preload on bolted joints leading to loosening.
risk.mit: Ensure proper torque application and use locking washers or inserts.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure due to overloading during catapult launch.
risk.mit: Conduct stress analysis and reinforce critical load paths.

risk.desc: Risk of water ingress through service access panel.
risk.mit: Use waterproof seals and gaskets on all access points.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to tolerance stack-up.
risk.mit: Implement precise manufacturing tolerances and ensure alignment fixtures are used.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.name: Load Path
physics_paths.path: Wing root -> Main spar -> Rib -> Fuselage frame

physics_paths.name: Thermal Path
physics_paths.path: Spar socket -> Wing skin -> Spar -> Wing trailing edge

physics_paths.name: Pressure Path
physics_paths.path: Wing leading edge -> Spar -> Wing trailing edge

physics_paths.name: Human Access Path
physics_paths.path: Battery bay -> Wing spar -> Wing trailing edge

physics_paths.name: Service Path
physics_paths.path: Wing spar -> Wing rib -> Wing trailing edge

integration_opportunities.description: Ensure spar socket aligns with wing root socket
Ensure rib mount aligns with wing rib
Ensure carry-through aligns with wing fuselage frame

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings

verification_focus.description: Verify lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure
Verify thermal paths and heat dissipation
Verify spar strength and stiffness under design loads

physics_paths.description: Lift and bending loads from spar to skin, distributed via rib nodes
Airflow over the wing rib, from leading edge to trailing edge
Heat transfer from sun to rib surface, then to core material
Hinge allows elevon to rotate for control surface actuation
Electrical signal from control hub to servo motor for elevon actuation

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate spar socket with rib core for improved load transfer

Refine winglet shape for enhanced lift and reduced drag

verification_focus.description: Verify load path continuity from spar to skin via rib nodes
Validate airflow over wing rib with CFD analysis
Test hinge line durability with cyclic actuation

risk.desc: Potential weak interface between motor mount and wing structure due to FDM process limitations.
risk.mit: Use reinforced inserts and ensure adequate wall thickness around the mounting holes.

risk.desc: Risk of cooling air intake obstruction due to complex geometry around the inlet.
risk.mit: Simplify the geometry around the cooling inlet to ensure clear airflow.

risk.desc: Propeller bolt pattern may not align perfectly with the propeller hub due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Increase the tolerance stack-up and ensure precise alignment during assembly.

risk.desc: Harness path may interfere with other components due to limited space in the motor pod.
risk.mit: Optimize the path of the harness to avoid interference with other components.

risk.desc: Service access panel may be difficult to remove due to tight tolerances and complex geometry.
risk.mit: Ensure the service access panel has a clear path for removal and consider adding access windows.

generated_parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
generated_parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

requirements: Design a forward downward camera gimbal bay for a fixed-wing flying-wing UAV with a 2m wingspan and 4+ hour endurance.
The bay should house a 2-axis micro gimbal and an optical window.
The subsystem must integrate with the wing structure, payload bay, and electrical harness.
Ensure the spar socket, anti-rotation keys, and center datum remain visible and inspectable.
Include EMI shielding, thermal dissipation path, and connector keying.
